data.q: Which PHP version officially introduced native 'Attributes' to replace PHPDoc annotations?
data.e: PHP 8.0 introduced native attributes, providing structured, type-safe metadata directly inside the language syntax rather than parsing docblock strings.

data.q: What is the correct syntax for declaring an attribute on a class or function in PHP?
data.e: PHP uses the `#[...]` syntax (the hash-bracket syntax) for attributes, which aligns with modern syntax preferences and doubles as a comment in older PHP versions to prevent syntax errors.

data.q: Which core engine class is used to fetch and inspect attributes via code?
data.e: The Reflection API was expanded in PHP 8.0 to include `ReflectionAttribute`, which allows developers to retrieve attributes from classes, methods, properties, and parameters.

data.q: To declare that a custom class can be used as an attribute, which attribute must be applied to it?
data.e: A class must be explicitly marked with the built-in `#[Attribute]` class attribute to be recognized and instantiated as a valid attribute container.

data.q: How do you pass arguments to an attribute when applying it to a structural element?
o: Using a JSON string inside the brackets
data.e: Attributes support standard PHP positional and named arguments, allowing you to pass configuration values directly during declaration.

o: An array of instantiated attribute objects
An array of string names of the attributes
A JSON representation of the metadata
data.e: `getAttributes()` returns an array of `ReflectionAttribute` elements. The actual custom attribute object is not instantiated until you explicitly call `newInstance()` on it.

data.q: Which flag can be passed to the `#[Attribute]` declaration to limit where it can be applied (e.g., only to properties)?
data.e: You can use bitwise flags like `Attribute::TARGET_PROPERTY`, `Attribute::TARGET_CLASS`, or `Attribute::TARGET_METHOD` to strictly control where an attribute is valid.

data.q: What is the default behavior regarding attribute inheritance if a parent class has an attribute?
o: Attributes are always automatically inherited by child classes
Attributes are ignored by child classes unless checked with the ReflectionAttribute::IS_INSTANCEOF flag in getAttributes()
PHP throws a compilation error if an attribute is inherited
Child classes must redeclare the parent's attributes exactly
data.e: By default, `getAttributes()` looks only at the specific target. To catch inherited attributes or implementations, pass `ReflectionAttribute::IS_INSTANCEOF` as an argument during retrieval.

data.q: How can you allow an attribute to be applied multiple times on the same target (e.g., repeating a route rule)?
o: By default, all attributes can be repeated
By passing Attribute::IS_REPEATABLE to the #[Attribute] definition
By separating declarations with a comma
data.e: To allow repeating an attribute on the same property or method, you must explicitly declare it with the bitwise flag: `#[Attribute(Attribute::IS_REPEATABLE)]`.

data.q: What happens if you look up an attribute name that doesn't exist as a class in the codebase using `getAttributes()`?
o: PHP crashes immediately with a Fatal Error
getAttributes() successfully returns a ReflectionAttribute instance, but calling newInstance() will throw an Error
The string name is returned instead of an object
data.e: Parsing attributes is deferred. Merely querying `getAttributes()` reads the raw text token data; the engine only attempts to check or instantiate the class when you run `newInstance()`.

data.q: Which of the following is a classic framework use-case for native PHP attributes?
o: Declaring environment variables in the .env file
Defining HTTP routing paths and validation constraints directly above controller methods
Compiling PHP code into fast binary targets
Managing connection pools inside a relational database
data.e: Modern PHP engines and frameworks (like Symfony and Laravel) rely heavily on attributes to cleanly associate routes, validation rules, or dependency injection hooks directly with the code structure.

data.q: Can attributes be applied to anonymous classes or closure expressions in PHP?
o: Yes, they can be applied to both anonymous classes and closure declarations
No, attributes are only valid for declared named structures
Only to anonymous classes, not to closures
Only to closures, not to anonymous classes
data.e: PHP's attribute engine allows attaching metadata to anonymous classes and arrow functions/closures, ensuring flexible metadata mapping even for dynamic structures.
